summary: Argentina's post-quantum activity centres on ARSAT, the state telecommunications company, which has developed CryptoComm, a post-quantum cryptographic system for secure messaging and file exchange, with a hardware version planned with INVAP. There is no national post-quantum standard, and the Second National Cybersecurity Strategy does not address post-quantum cryptography.
govActors: ARSAT | State telecommunications company, developed the CryptoComm post-quantum secure-communications system
provenance: summary + govActors (ARSAT): the two ARSAT candidates (CryptoComm announcement and the 2022 secure-communications page, arsat.com.ar). Both are T2 and included:false, so confirm inclusion before treating as settled - REVIEW
dominantProcess OMITTED: CryptoComm is a single state enterprise's product, not a national scheme or standards camp, and no FIPS suite or scheme is named; grey beats wrong
standardFamilies / algorithms / hybridDeployment / migrationTimeline / targetCompletion OMITTED: none stated in institutional sources

Second National Cybersecurity Strategy candidate was WebFetch-confirmed NOT to mention post-quantum cryptography; National Cybersecurity Directorate/Centre and BCRA IT-risk rules name no PQC; Cyber.Ar 2026 congress and opinion/academic pieces are non-institutional - all excluded. Recommend re-scrape with --deep for a national-authority PQC source

dominantProcess re-classified NIST by best-available signal (low): ARSAT's CryptoComm is a single state-enterprise PQC product rather than a national algorithm scheme, and Argentina has no national PQC standard; the engagement defaults to the de facto international (NIST) camp. Caveat: CryptoComm's bespoke symmetric design is idiosyncratic, so the signal is weak - provisional, flagged for review
Model 2026-06-14 (engaged-unaligned, Medium): De-facto NIST default on thin attestation; no sourced national adoption. Engaged but uncommitted pending evidence.
